programme.title: C'est bon à savoir
programme.desc: "C'est bon à savoir" décrypte les bénéfices pour la santé de la consommation des fruits et légumes et analyse leur mode de culture, sans oublier de parler d'environnement. Avec la complicité d'experts, ce programme revient quelques idées reçues.

programme.title: Météo régionale
programme.desc: Un bulletin météorologique propre à chaque région.

programme.title: Stade 2
programme.desc: Chaque fin de semaine, Stade 2 propose de retrouver les résultats des derniers événements sportifs, mais aussi des portraits, des grands reportages, des interviews en plateau ou en duplex et des enquêtes. Au programme : du football, du rugby, du tennis, du cyclisme, des sports mécaniques, et bien d'autres disciplines.

programme.title: Vanina Meurtres en Sicile
programme.desc: Pendant la fête de Sant'Agata, patronne de la ville de Catane, Vasco Nocera est retrouvé mort dans un des carrosses d'époque exposés au public. Rentier ayant hérité du patrimoine de son père, qui était autrefois l'avocat d'une riche famille de la région, il ne travaillait pas et semblait même dilapider sa fortune au détriment de sa famille. Vanina mène l'enquête et l'aide de Biagio Patanè, commissaire à la retraite, est une fois de plus indispensable.

programme.title: Vanina Meurtres en Sicile
programme.desc: Vanina reçoit un coup de fil anonyme d'une femme prétendant qu'un crime a été commis dans une villa et que le cadavre a été placé dans une valise. Vanina n'y croit pas trop, mais un appel de Manfredi la fait douter. La nuit précédente, il a été témoin d'une scène étrange juste en bas de chez lui : un homme a jeté une valise à l'eau, qui s'est échouée sur les rochers. Quand la criminelle arrive sur les lieux, elle découvre la valise vide, à part des traces de sang et un portable. Le téléphone appartient à une certaine Lorenza Lannino, une avocate qui semble avoir disparu.

programme.title: Vanina Meurtres en Sicile
programme.desc: Le cadavre d'un homme est découvert dans la grotte d'une rivière souterraine utilisée comme salle de bar. Vincenzo La Barbera, professeur de philosophie, venait d'une famille extrêmement riche, mais avait renoncé à son héritage et vivait sur un vieux voilier amarré au port. La Barbera était un idéaliste, mais surtout, c'était un courageux militant qui luttait contre le trafic de drogue. À tel point que la mafia l'avait pris pour cible. Pourtant, le meurtre n'a pas les caractéristiques habituelles d'un crime mafieux.

programme.title: Journal Météo climat

programme.title: Apocalypse : Les débarquements
programme.desc: Printemps 44: Les Alliés veulent mettre fin à la domination d'Hitler sur l'Europe occidentale. En Angleterre, les troupes sont entraînées dans le secret pour une immense opération amphibie. Alors que tout est prêt, la météo oblige le commandement à reporter l'opération, mettant son succès en péril.

programme.title: Questions pour un champion
programme.desc: Un jeu réalisé par Olivier Baudoin et Karl Grosse Produit par Fremantle, avec la participation de France 3 Animé par Samuel Etienne Jeu emblématique construit autour de la culture générale, l'émission culte "Questions pour un champion" se déroule en trois manches : - Le "9 points gagnants" lors de laquelle la rapidité est de mise. - Le "4 à la suite" où dans un temps de 40 secondes maximum, le but est de donner 4 bonnes réponses à la suite sur un thème choisi. - Et le "Face-à-face

programme.title: Le goût des rencontres normandes
programme.desc: Le chef étoilé David Gallienne va se plonger dans l'esprit mystique et naturel du village de La-Haye-de-Routot dans l'Eure. C'est au cœur d'une prairie qu'il va faire la connaissance de Marie, une botaniste. Embauchée il y a dix ans par l'association Terres Vivantes, qui gère plusieurs musées du village, elle anime des ateliers cueillette-cuisine dans la chaumière aux orties.

programme.title: ICI Matin
programme.desc: De 6h30 à 9h, " Ici Matin " est votre premier rendez-vous de la journée avec l'information de proximité. Elle ouvre les matinées 100 % régionales de France 3. Au programme, des journaux d'information en hyper proximité tous les quarts d'heure, des points météo et trafic réguliers, de l'interactivité avec les auditeurs et téléspectateurs, et surtout une grande bouffée de bonne humeur pour démarrer la journée du bon pied !

programme.title: On vous emmène ici
programme.desc: Une production par les rédactions régionales de France 3 Présenté en alternance par Raphaëlle Tavernier et Grégoire Bouscambert Chaque jour, on prendra le temps… Le temps d'un reportage pour raconter nos territoires, s'arrêter dans un site remarquable, un lieu emblématique, un patrimoine à découvrir.

programme.title: ICI dans votre région
programme.desc: Des magazines ou documentaires proposés par votre antenne régionale.

programme.title: La p'tite librairie
programme.desc: Une émission proposée et écrite par François Busnel Produite par Rosebud Productions avec la participation de France Télévisions François Busnel propose chaque jour de vous faire découvrir ou redécouvrir une œuvre française ou étrangère : roman, essai, grand classique ou encore bande dessinée.

programme.title: Le goût des rencontres normandes
programme.desc: Le Chef David Gallienne part à la rencontre des hommes et des femmes de la Normandie, à l'écoute de leurs histoires, à la découverte des produits, des goûts afin de préparer de délicieuses recettes à partager.

programme.title: ICI 12/13 - Edition toute région
programme.desc: Le rendez-vous de la mi-journée des 24 rédactions régionales de France Télévisions, avec les journaux locaux, les reportages propres à chaque région, la météo, mais aussi un regard de proximité sur l'actualité nationale et internationale.

programme.title: Météo à la carte
programme.desc: Une émission réalisée par Captain Productions Une production Enibas Productions, A Prime Group, avec la participation de France Télévisions Rédacteurs en chef : Stéphane Evanno et Laetitia Bouchon Présentée par Marine Vignes et Laurent Romejko En direct "Météo à la carte

programme.title: Météo à la carte (la suite)
programme.desc: Une émission réalisée par Captain Productions Une production Enibas Productions, A Prime Group, avec la participation de France Télévisions Rédacteurs en chef : Stéphane Evanno et Laetitia Bouchon Présentée par Marine Vignes et Laurent Romejko En direct Ce nouveau format se veut encore plus interactif. Les téléspectateurs gardent une place importante dans l'émission et sur les réseaux. Les photos et vidéos envoyées vont être davantage mises en valeur.

programme.title: Tour Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes
programme.desc: Pour cette deuxième étape, tracée entre Saint‑Martin‑le‑Vinoux et Le Puy‑en‑Velay, le peloton du Tour Auvergne-Rhône-alpes traverse quatre départements : l'Isère, la Drôme, l'Ardèche et la Haute‑Loire. Trois principales difficultés jalonnent le parcours : le col Robert‑Marchand, la côte des Baraques et, pour finir, la côte de Saint‑Vidal.

programme.title: Duels en familles
programme.desc: Un jeu réalisé par Ben Cap Produit par CYRILPROD et A Prime Group Dans un décor d'appartement géant, deux familles de quatre membres viennent défendre les couleurs de leur région en s'affrontant chaque jour de la semaine, au cours de trois duels et une finale. Au programme : duels en images, duels en régions, duels de mots, duels en musique, duels en culture … Chaque jour, la famille victorieuse se qualifie pour la finale pour tenter de remporter 2000 €. Le vendredi, pour le dernier match de la semaine, c'est une grande finale à 5000 € qui attend les candidats. Quelle sera la famille et donc la région gagnante de la semaine ?

programme.title: Slam
programme.desc: Un jeu réalisé par Antoine Galey, Patricia Rimond des Anges et Philippe Letourneur Produit par Effervescence Label, avec la participation de France 3 Animé par Théo Curin Accessible à tous les publics, "Slam" est un jeu de lettres 100 % original qui fait la joie des amoureux des mots, des férus de quiz et des adeptes des mots fléchés, en mixant tous les genres, de la géographie aux mathématiques, en passant par la chimie... et bien sûr le français. Au menu : vivacité d'esprit, humour, vocabulaire, orthographe et bien sûr culture générale !

programme.title: Un si grand soleil
programme.desc: Boris a une grande nouvelle à annoncer à sa famille. Quant à Salomé, elle ne compte pas se laisser faire.

programme.title: Un si grand soleil
programme.desc: Alors que Johanna est touchée par la situation de Salomé, Catherine est bien décidée à empêcher Boris de commettre une erreur.

programme.title: Qui m'aime me suive!
programme.desc: Gilbert et Simone vivent une retraite agitée dans un village du Sud de la France. Le départ d'Étienne, son voisin et amant, le manque d'argent, mais surtout l'aigreur permanente de son mari, poussent Simone à fuir le foyer. Gilbert prend alors conscience qu'il est prêt à tout pour retrouver sa femme, son amour.

programme.title: Journal Météo climat

programme.title: La Recluse de Saint-Flour, contre-enquête
programme.desc: En octobre 1983, le GIGN découvre à Saint-Flour, une femme recluse depuis près de quatre décennies : Esther Albouy; elle aurait été enfermée par sa propre famille.

programme.title: Antilles : la mer en héritage
programme.desc: L'histoire des Antilles reflète un lien ambivalent avec la mer. En Martinique et en Guadeloupe, environ 30% de la population ne sait pas nager. L'économie, centrée sur l'agriculture d'exportation, a relégué les activités maritimes au second plan, laissant la pêche artisanale sous-développée. Aujourd'hui, une réconciliation s'opère, portée par une jeune génération tournée vers les métiers de la mer et les enjeux environnementaux.

programme.title: TED
programme.sub-title: Marco Tempest: A cyber-magic card trick like no other
programme.desc: The suits, numbers and colors in a deck of cards correspond to the seasons, moon cycles and calendar. Marco Tempest straps on augmented reality goggles and does a card trick like you’ve never seen before, weaving a lyrical tale as he deals. (This version fixes a glitch in the original performance, but is otherwise exactly as seen live by the TEDGlobal audience, including the dazzling augmented reality effects.)TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: Gran Dillama

programme.title: TED
programme.sub-title: Ernesto Sirolli: Want to help someone? Shut up and listen!
programme.desc: When most well-intentioned aid workers hear of a problem they think they can fix, they go to work. This, Ernesto Sirolli suggests, is naïve. In this funny and impassioned talk, he proposes that the first step is to listen to the people you're trying to help, and tap into their own entrepreneurial spirit. His advice on what works will help any entrepreneur.TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: Til Israel bomber igjen
programme.desc: Til Israel bomber igjen.

programme.title: TED
programme.sub-title: Sanjay Pradhan: How open data is changing international aid
programme.desc: How do we make sure that development and aid money actually goes to the people who most need it? Sanjay Pradhan of the World Bank Institute lays out three guidelines to help relief efforts make the most impact — while curbing corruption. One key: connecting the players who are working to change broken systems with the data they need.TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: TED
programme.sub-title: Andy Yen: Think your email's private? Think again
programme.desc: Sending an email message is like sending a postcard, says scientist Andy Yen in this thought-provoking talk: Anyone can read it. Yet encryption, the technology that protects the privacy of email communication, does exist. It's just that until now it has been difficult to install and a hassle to use. Showing a demo of an email program he designed with colleagues at CERN, Yen argues that encryption can be made simple to the point of becoming the default option, providing true email privacy to all.TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: Medieovervåkerne

programme.title: TED
programme.desc: Comic author Rob Reid unveils Copyright Math (TM), a remarkable new field of study based on actual numbers from entertainment industry lawyers and lobbyists.TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: Diesen og Otte: Hvordan Tyskland ødela seg selv
programme.desc: Diesen og Otte: Hvordan Tyskland ødela seg selv.

programme.title: TED
programme.sub-title: Christopher Soghoian: A brief history of phone wiretapping -- and how to avoid it
programme.desc: Who is listening in on your phone calls? On a landline, it could be anyone, says privacy activist Christopher Soghoian, because surveillance backdoors are built into the phone system by default, to allow governments to listen in. But then again, so could a foreign intelligence service ... or a criminal. Which is why, says Soghoian, some tech companies are resisting governments' call to build the same backdoors into mobile phones and new messaging systems. Learn how some tech companies are working to keep your calls and messages private.TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: TED
programme.sub-title: John Green: The nerd's guide to learning everything online
programme.desc: Some of us learn best in the classroom, and some of us ... well, we don't. But we still love to learn, to find out new things about the world and challenge our minds. We just need to find the right place to do it, and the right community to learn with. In this charming talk, author John Green shares the world of learning he found in online video.TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.sub-title: Dan Ariely: How equal do we want the world to be? You'd be surprised
programme.desc: The news of society's growing inequality makes all of us uneasy. But why? Dan Ariely reveals some new, surprising research on what we think is fair, as far as how wealth is distributed over societies ... then shows how it stacks up to the real stats.TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.sub-title: Ken Robinson: How to escape education's death valley
programme.desc: Sir Ken Robinson outlines 3 principles crucial for the human mind to flourish -- and how current education culture works against them. In a funny, stirring talk he tells us how to get out of the educational "death valley" we now face, and how to nurture our youngest generations with a climate of possibility.TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: TED
programme.sub-title: James Veitch: This is what happens when you reply to spam email
programme.desc: Suspicious emails: unclaimed insurance bonds, diamond-encrusted safe deposit boxes, close friends marooned in a foreign country. They pop up in our inboxes, and standard procedure is to delete on sight. But what happens when you reply? Follow along as writer and comedian James Veitch narrates a hilarious, months-long exchange with a spammer who offered to cut him in on a hot deal.TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.desc: How do you read a two-thousand-year-old manuscript that has been erased, cut up, written on and painted over? With a powerful particle accelerator, of course! programme.title: TED
programme.sub-title: Andreas Ekström: The moral bias behind your search results
programme.desc: Search engines have become our most trusted sources of information and arbiters of truth. But can we ever get an unbiased search result? Swedish author and journalist Andreas Ekström argues that such a thing is a philosophical impossibility. In this thoughtful talk, he calls on us to strengthen the bonds between technology and the humanities, and he reminds us that behind every algorithm is a set of personal beliefs that no code can ever completely eradicate.TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: Practical Protocol-Oriented Programming in Swift
programme.desc: Value types are at the core of Swift (seriously, mostly everything in the Swift standard library is a value type). But how do you avoid subclassing? That's where the power of Protocol-Oriented programming comes in. Learn how to structure your code to (almost) never subclass again! Practical everyday examples and ideas for your own code base will be included.The speaker Natasha Murashev is an iOS developer by day and a robot by night. She blogs about Swift, WatchOS, and iOS development on her blog, natashatherobot.com, and curates a fast-growing weekly Swift newsletter: This Week in Swift. In her free time, she works on personal projects, speaks at meetups and conferences, contributes to open source, and likes to cross things off her bucket list. She's currently living the digital nomad life as her alter identity: @NatashaTheNomad.

programme.title: TED
programme.sub-title: Bruce Feiler: Agile programming -- for your family
programme.desc: Bruce Feiler has a radical idea: To deal with the stress of modern family life, go agile. Inspired by agile software programming, Feiler introduces family practices which encourage flexibility, bottom-up idea flow, constant feedback and accountability. One surprising feature: Kids pick their own punishments.TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: TED
programme.sub-title: David Pizarro: The strange politics of disgust
programme.desc: What does a disgusting image have to do with how you vote? Equipped with surveys and experiments, psychologist David Pizarro demonstrates a correlation between sensitivity to disgusting cues -- a photo of feces, an unpleasant odor -- and moral and political conservatism. (Filmed at TEDxEast.)TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: MakerCon
programme.sub-title: Hovering Clouds: Looking at online tool offerings for Product Design and 3D Printing
programme.desc: William Kempton at MakerCon Nordic 2014-10-18.

programme.title: TED
programme.sub-title: Tania Simoncelli: How I took on the gene patent industry -- and won
programme.desc: Are human genes patentable? Back in 2005, when Tania Simoncelli first contemplated this complex question, US patent law said they were — which meant patent holders had the right to stop anyone from sequencing, testing or even looking at a patented gene. Troubled by the way this law both harmed patients and created a barrier to biomedical innovation, Simoncelli and her colleagues at the ACLU challenged it. In this riveting talk, hear the story of how they took a case everybody told them they would lose all the way to the Supreme Court.TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: Diesen og Jiang: Iran-krigen og kampen for petrodollar
programme.desc: Diesen og Jiang: Iran-krigen og kampen for petrodollar.

programme.title: Blikk på historien. Kanaans land og Palestina
programme.desc: Trond Ali forteller om Kanaans land og Palestina.

programme.title: TED
programme.sub-title: James B. Glattfelder: Who controls the world?
programme.desc: James Glattfelder studies complexity: how an interconnected system -- say, a swarm of birds -- is more than the sum of its parts. And complexity theory, it turns out, can reveal a lot about how the economy works. Glattfelder shares a groundbreaking study of how control flows through the global economy, and how concentration of power in the hands of a shockingly small number leaves us all vulnerable. (Filmed at TEDxZurich.)TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: TED
programme.desc: Using sleight-of-hand techniques and charming storytelling, illusionist Marco Tempest brings a jaunty stick figure to life onstage at TEDGlobal.TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: TED
programme.sub-title: Marco Tempest: The magic of truth and lies (and iPods)
programme.desc: Using three iPods like magical props, Marco Tempest spins a clever, surprisingly heartfelt meditation on truth and lies, art and emotion.TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: Diesen spør Putin om Sverige & Finland i Nato

programme.title: TED
programme.sub-title: Apollo Robbins: The art of misdirection
programme.desc: Hailed as the greatest pickpocket in the world, Apollo Robbins studies the quirks of human behavior as he steals your watch. In a hilarious demonstration, Robbins samples the buffet of the TEDGlobal 2013 audience, showing how the flaws in our perception make it possible to swipe a wallet and leave it on its owner’s shoulder while they remain clueless.TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: Hvordan går det med Anita og Johnny? Mye har skjedd med disse tidligere vitnene
programme.desc: None.

programme.title: TED
programme.sub-title: Chris Urmson: How a driverless car sees the road
programme.desc: Statistically, the least reliable part of the car is ... the driver. Chris Urmson heads up Google's driverless car program, one of several efforts to remove humans from the driver's seat. He talks about where his program is right now, and shares fascinating footage that shows how the car sees the road and makes autonomous decisions about what to do next.TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: Åpning av Frikanalen
programme.desc: Opptak fra livesendingen på Folkets Hus 8.januar.Trond Giske Åpner den første ordinære sendingen, samt en breakdance til en strykekvartett akkopagnert av en trommis. Produsert av unge frivillige fra The Gathering under ledelse av Andreas Aanerud :+).

programme.title: TED
programme.sub-title: Nancy Lublin: Texting that saves lives
programme.desc: When Nancy Lublin started texting teenagers to help with her social advocacy organization, what she found was shocking -- they started texting back about their own problems, from bullying to depression to abuse. So she's setting up a text-only crisis line, and the results might be even more important than she expected.TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: TED
programme.desc: Paved roads are nice to look at, but they’re easily damaged and costly to repair. Erik Schlangen demos a new type of porous asphalt made of simple materials with an astonishing feature: When cracked, it can be “healed” by induction heating. (Filmed at TEDxDelft.)TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: TED
programme.sub-title: Nathalie Cabrol: How Mars might hold the secret to the origin of life
programme.desc: While we like to imagine little green men, it’s far more likely that life on other planets will be microbial. Planetary scientist Nathalie Cabrol takes us inside the search for microbes on Mars, a hunt which counterintuitively leads us to the remote lakes of the Andes mountains. This extreme environment — with its thin atmosphere and scorched land — approximates the surface of Mars about 3.5 billion years ago. How microbes adapt to survive here may just show us where to look on Mars — and could help us understand why some microbial pathways lead to civilization while others are a dead end.TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: TED
programme.sub-title: Keith Chen: Could your language affect your ability to save money?
programme.desc: What can economists learn from linguists? Behavioral economist Keith Chen introduces a fascinating pattern from his research: that languages without a concept for the future -- "It rain tomorrow," instead of "It will rain tomorrow" -- correlate strongly with high savings rates. Read more about Chen’s explorations »TED talk downloaded from The Internet Archive.

programme.title: Vi på Saltbrygga
programme.desc: I denne episoden blir det høy puls på Saltbrygga. Det sosiale er viktig for beboerne og det innebærer sprell, fest og pubkveld! Men er alle like entusiastiske?

programme.title: Elektrikerne
programme.desc: Christian havner i et hekstisk hasteoppdrag i Hammerfest, mens Terje sjekker og bytter lys i skibakken før vinterstart.

programme.title: Boligrådet
programme.desc: Vi får toneangivende røster i studio når leder i Leieboerforeningen snakker om krise i leiemarkedet og vanskeligstilte leieboere.

programme.title: Herlige hjem
programme.desc: Lise har designet huset i fjellheimen helt selv; alt av interiør, planløsning og materialbruk er nøye gjennomtenkt. Vi besøker også en familie på tre blant fjell og vakker natur på Vågå. De er glade i den nordiske stilen med hjemmesnekrede møbler, lyse farger og naturmaterialer.

programme.title: Flipp med Emilie
programme.desc: Emilie og Håvard finner det perfekte flippeobjektet, og det er duket for en intens budrunde. Kommer de til å klare å holde hodet kaldt, med så mye annet som skjer rundt seg?

programme.title: Boligrådet
programme.desc: Boligmarkedet er i krise, men hvor ille står det egentlig til? Vi får svarene.

programme.title: Nabolaget
programme.sub-title: En klassisk Oslo leilighet på Fredenborg
programme.desc: Vi drar på besøk hos klesdesigner og gründer Camilla Volden, kjent for klesmerket Confettibird. På Fredensborg bor hun i en klassisk Oslo-leilighet med stukkatur og høyt til taket, et rolig nabolag bare et steinkast fra sentrum.

programme.title: Boligrådet
programme.desc: Vi skal ut i hagen, inn på soverommet og få dagsaktuelle bolignyheter i dagens episode.

programme.desc: Vi drar på besøk hos klesdesigner og gründer Camilla Volden, kjent for klesmerket Confettibird. På Fredensborg bor hun i en klassisk Oslo-leilighet med stukkatur og høyt til taket, et rolig nabolag bare et steinkast fra sentrum.

programme.title: Boligrådet
programme.desc: Vi skal ut i hagen, inn på soverommet og få dagsaktuelle bolignyheter i dagens episode.

programme.title: Rom for Suksess
programme.sub-title: Tre faser med tidløst design - Skøyen atrium
programme.desc: I denne episoden besøker vi Skøyen Atrium, et vakkert næringskompleks fylt med moderne kunst, som har vokst frem langs Drammensveien i tre faser.

programme.title: Boligrådet
programme.desc: Hvordan står det til i leiemarkedet? Hva er vårens interiørtrender og hvorfor gjør de seg gjeldende nå?

programme.title: Bygningsarven
programme.desc: Bli med på kulturvandring i Arendals skjærgård, med historiefortelling om byens storhetsperiode under seilskutetiden og besøk i den gamle uthvnen Merdø.

programme.title: Elektrikerne
programme.desc: Christian havner i et hekstisk hasteoppdrag i Hammerfest, mens Terje sjekker og bytter lys i skibakken før vinterstart.

programme.title: Boligrådet
programme.desc: Hvilke kvaliteter har gamle bygg og hvorfor er det viktige å bevare?

programme.title: Herlige hjem
programme.desc: Norges fremste interiørdesigner og stylist Tone Kroken er opptatt av å skape et trendy hjem med mye sjel som reflekterer hvem hun er. Grunder av populære «Milla Boutique» Camilla Berntsen har pusset opp den tidligere 300 kvm store svinestallen på tunet til gården hun kjøpte med samboer manager Henrik Bhamra for fem år siden. Vi møter dem begge i denne episoden.

programme.title: Clash of the Gods
programme.desc: Han var den norrøne guden som beskyttet og kjempet mot jotnene og Midgardsormen. Men tror historikerne at han har levd? Nye funn avslører sannheten bak tordenguden Tor.

programme.title: Hunting Hitler
programme.sub-title: Recap Special - Anatomy of a Manhunt
programme.desc: Bob Baer og hans team av eliteetterforskere gir oss en nærmere titt på det de mener er Adolf Hitlers sanne skjebne.

programme.title: Grant
programme.desc: I en av de mest utrolige fortellingene fra amerikansk historie går Ulysses S. Grant fra fattige kår til å bli generalen som vinner et av borgerkrigens blodigste slag.

programme.title: The Best of MonsterQuest
programme.desc: Hver episode tar for seg en mystisk skapning. Enkelte er basert på virkeligheten, og andre kan være fri fantasi. Men noen er resultatet av en skremmende skapelsesdrøm.

programme.title: Project Impossible
programme.desc: En revolusjon innen ingeniørkunsten vil gjøre dagens teknologi like utdatert som hest og kjerre. I denne episoden: Lightning LS-218, verdens raskeste motorsykkel, og Hyperloop, toget som overgår lydhastigheten.

programme.title: Unidentified: Inside America's UFO Investigation
programme.desc: En tidligere radaroperatør i den amerikanske marinen står frem for å fortelle om den skremmende dagen da det regnet UFO-er. Marinepersonell avslører flere detaljer og forklarer hvorfor man ser UFO-er over havene.

programme.title: The Unxplained With William Shatner
programme.desc: Millioner av mennesker over hele verden hevder å ha sett skapninger som ikke finnes ifølge tradisjonell vitenskap, som møllmannen, chupacabra og den mongolske dødsormen.

programme.desc: En tidligere radaroperatør i den amerikanske marinen står frem for å fortelle om den skremmende dagen da det regnet UFO-er. Marinepersonell avslører flere detaljer og forklarer hvorfor man ser UFO-er over havene.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Ross vil finne ut mer om oldefaren og sjømannen Arthur 'Pop' Chalmers. Han oppdager at Pop tjenestegjorde i handelsflåten og overlevde tre forlis. For å finne ut mer treffer han marinarkeologen Mallory Haas.

programme.title: Lost Worlds
programme.desc: I 1939 advarte en gruppe forskere FDR om at Tyskland kanskje prøvde å lage en atombombe. Roosevelt ga ordre om at USA måtte skape det første våpenet. Vi gjenskaper en skjult verden av hemmelige atomvåpenfabrikker.

programme.title: Cities of the Underworld
programme.desc: Under de moderne bygatene finnes det eldgamle underjordiske kvarterer og kloakksystemer. De vitner om tekniske underverker som fremskyndet imperiets utvikling.

programme.title: Evolusjon
programme.desc: Evnen til å reagere og forflytte seg kan bety forskjellen på liv og død i dyreriket. Enkelte dyr er mesterlige flygere, svømmere og løpere.

programme.title: Engineering Disasters
programme.desc: Hvorfor krasjlandet Asiana flight 214 med passasjerer og snurret rundt over hele rullebanen på San Francisco International Airport? Tre av familiene til de omkomne vil ha svar.

programme.title: Ancient Top 10
programme.desc: Oldtidens store riker ble skapt gjennom krig, og de største slagene omfattet beleiringer. Fra den legendariske trojanerkrigen til Romerrikets fall. Hvilken beleiring topper lista som den mest ødeleggende?

programme.title: Life After People
programme.desc: I en verden blottet for mennesker begynner pytonslanger å utfordre alligatorer for kontroll over Everglades. Andre steder synker Shanghai under vekten av sine tomme skyskrapere.

programme.title: Brad Meltzer's Lost History
programme.desc: Programleder Brad Meltzer og seerne er på jakt etter historie som har gått tapt eller er stjålet, blant annet en historisk minneplate for Alex Haleys serie Røtter, et ekte eksemplar av grunnloven og en mystisk ring som tilhørte Lee Harvey Oswald.

programme.title: De største Imperiene: Slik ble de bygget
programme.desc: På under 200 år gikk aztekerne fra å være en nomadestamme til å bli den største sivilisasjonen Den nye verden hadde sett. Hvordan klarte de det?

programme.title: Cities of the Underworld
programme.desc: Moskva er en by viden kjent for sine hensynsløse ledere og tallrike hemmeligheter. Under byens gater ligger restene etter en krigsherjet fortid og den grusomme arven etter Stalin.

programme.title: Where Did It Come From?
programme.desc: For 2000 år siden, da Washington fortsatt var en sump, gjorde mayafolket den ubeboelige jungelen til den nye verdens første storby.

programme.title: The Universe
programme.desc: Se hvordan sola både kan være nyttig og skadelig for livet på jorda.

programme.title: Ancient Aliens
programme.desc: Gudene som vikingene tilba var fryktinngytende og mystiske. Eksperter undersøker om de mektige vikinggudene kan ha kommet fra andre planeter.

programme.title: Ancient Discoveries
programme.desc: Oppfinneren Al-Jazari døde i 1206 e. Kr. og sto bak en rekke konstruksjoner. Utforsk hans bidrag til den islamske ingeniørhistorien.

programme.title: Clash of the Gods
programme.desc: Fortellingen om Medusa, den mest beryktede kvinnen i gresk mytologi, som med bare blikket forvandlet alt til stein. Men hva er den sanne historien bak myten? Finnes det spor på nattehimmelen som viser opphavet til fortellingen?

programme.title: Life After People
programme.desc: Oppdag skjebnen til menneskehetens krigsmaskineri i en verden uten folk, der Pearl Harbor blir utsatt for fornyede angrep, og snø forårsaker urbane snøskred.

programme.title: London Symphony Orchestra, Sir Antonio Pappano: Tchaikovsky, Vaughan Williams
programme.desc: London Symphony Chorus. Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikosvky 1840 - 1893 Symphony No 4. Ralph Vaughan Williams 1872 - 1958 Flos Campi, for Viola & Chorus Dona nobis pacem. London, Barbican Hall, 7 December 2025.

programme.title: InterMezzo
programme.desc: Espacio que incluye la emisión de una selección de los mejores momentos musicales. Una manera diferente y muy variada de descubrir la música, los intérpretes y las orquestas.

programme.title: Berlioz: La Damnation de Faust - Pene Pati, Aude Extremo - Monte-Carlo
programme.desc: La Damnation de Faust‘Légende dramatique’ in four parts by Hector Berlioz (1803-1869)Libretto by Hector Berlioz and Almire Gandonnière after Gérard de Nerval’s translation of ‘Faust’ by Johann Wolfgang von GoetheFirst performance in Paris, Opéra-Comique, December 6, 1846.

programme.title: Nielsen: Symphony no. 5 - Danish National Symphony Orchestra, Fabio Luisi
programme.desc: Carl Nielsen 1865- 1931 Violin Concerto Op. 33 Symphony No. 5, Op. 50. Copenhagen, DR Koncerthuset, 2023 Directed by Kasper Lange.

programme.title: Kokoroko - Nancy Jazz Pulsations
programme.desc: KokorokoSetlist :TojoBaba AyoolaDide OhAge of ascentOye AsemNo ConditionCarry me HomeWar DanceSomething’s going onWe give thanksNancy Jazz Pulsations Festival 2022, October 12, 2022Directed by Sylvain Pierrel.

programme.title: Stochelo Rosenberg: Gyspy Today - Nancy Jazz Pulsations 2022
programme.desc: Stochelo Rosenberg (guitar), Jermaine Landsberger (piano), Guido May (drums), William Brunard (double bass)Set listAnouman, Django ReinhardtDouble Scotch, Django ReinhardtMade for Izaak, Stochelo RosenbergSeptember Song, Kurt WeilMemories of Bridget, Stochelo RosenbergDouble Game, Stochelo RosenbergPoinciana, Ahmad JamalGypsylogy, Jermaine LandsbergerSeresta, Paquito D'RiveraGypsy Today, Jermaine LandsbergerNancy, Salle Poirel (Festival Nancy Jazz Pulsations 2022), October 7, 2022.TV production Sylvain Pierrel.

programme.title: Joaquín Achúcarro: Chopin, Grieg, Brahms, Rachmaninov, Scriabin, Liszt - Guggenheim Bilbao
programme.desc: Frédéric Chopin 1810 - 1849 Sonate pour piano n° 3 en si mineur, op. 58 . Edvard Grieg 1843-1907 Pièces lyriques, op. 54 n° 4 : Notturno . Johannes Brahms 1833-1897 Intermezzo en la majeur, op. 118 n° 2 Intermezzo en si bémol mineur, op. 117 n° 2 Intermezzo en mi bémol majeur, op. 117 n° 1. Sergueï Rachmaninov 1873-1943 Prélude en fa dièse mineur, op. 23 n° 1 Prélude en sol bémol majeur, op. 23 n° 10 Prélude en si mineur, op. 32 n° 12 Prélude en mi mineur, op. 32 n° 4. Alexandre Scriabine 1872-1915 Étude en ré dièse mineur, op. 8 n° 12 ‘Pathétique’ Étude en do dièse mineur, op. 2 n° 1. Franz Liszt 1811-1886 Valse oubliée n° 1, S.215/1 Notturno n° 3 ‘Liebesträume’ en la bémol majeur, S.541/3. Réalisé par Ángela Álvarez Rilla Bilbao, Museo Guggenheim, 2022 Concert classique.

programme.title: Massenet : Thaïs - Opéra de Monte-Carlo
programme.desc: Thaïs'Comédie lyrique' in three acts by Jules Massenet (1842 – 1912)Libretto by Louis Gallet based on the novel by Anatole FranceFirst performance in Paris, Opéra, 16 March 1894.

programme.title: Opera Now: György Kurtág
programme.desc: OPERA NOW is a six-part documentary series hosted by Vasco Mendonça, a composer with a passion for contemporary opera who is committed to introducing the general public to this unique world. In each episode, he presents a landmark work of the 21st century through interviews with composers, directors, and singers... . In 1957, Samuel Beckett’s play Fin de Partie, written in French, premiered. A year later, its English-language version followed, and half a century later, György Kurtág adapted it into an opera! Fin de Partie : scènes et monologues, a one-act opera, thus features the four characters from the original play, engaged in an obscure dialogue: is a tragedy unfolding before our eyes, or is this a strange daily ritual? Dive into this episode of OPERA NOW to find out more….

programme.title: Bruno Walter . Portrait
programme.desc: The film portrays Bruno Walter (1876-1962), one of the most important conductors of the 20th century. The Italian biographer Michele Selvini speaks about the musician Bruno Walter who formed the musical life of the capitals of Europe and the United States over a period of 60 years. But so far not published film material also shows a different, not so known Bruno Walter as a private man.

programme.title: Unlimited Miles - Blue Note Tokyo
programme.desc: Celebrating the 100th anniversary of the birth of Miles Davis, this dream project brings together an all-star sextet led by pianist and composer John Beasley, who joined Miles Davis' band in his twenties. Joined by Sean Jones, Kurt Rosenwinkel, Mark Turner, Ben Williams, Terreon Gully, and Beasley, the ensemble interpret Miles's music with their signature grooves, bold harmonies,.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: L'humoriste et animateur Jim Moir et sa femme Nancy Sorrell partent en road trip dans le Kent aujourd'hui.Mieux connu sous le nom de Vic Reeves, Jim apporte sa touche d'humour unique à l'achat d'antiquités. De son côté, Nancy, mannequin et présentatrice télé, sait aussi marchander et elle est très compétitive ! Le couple est accompagné par les experts Charlie Ross et Thomas Plant.Soyez attentif au don surprenant de Jim pour deviner l’âge de presque tout, à Dixon traversant les chutes du Niagara et à une leçon de navigation par longitude.Et surtout, la mystérieuse Mme Parker fera-t-elle son apparition à la vente aux enchères ?

programme.title: Photo Contest
programme.desc: In each episode of Photo Contest, two contestants compete on an imposed theme such as rock'n roll, Japan or architecture. They have one hour to take the best possible photographs before editing them for 30 minutes in a studio. The photographs they have selected are then presented to Agnès Grégoire, editor-in-chief of PHOTO magazine, who will have to choose the best photographer.

programme.title: Artist's Workshop
programme.desc: In the Artist's workshop series, we offer you a privileged meeting with an artist, or the discovery of an artist or an important place for the history of art. Whether they are painters, visual artists, sculptors... These people all have the same passion for art and this show is an opportunity to enter their different universes, to discover what drives them, inspires them and maybe that will give rise to vocations.

programme.title: An hour with
programme.desc: Take an hour break to discover the work of a French painter, Auguste Renoir. You will discover his labour with Girls at the piano, The Bathers, still-life with apples and pears.

programme.title: An hour with
programme.desc: Take an hour break to discover the work of an Italian painter, Raphaël. You will discover his labour with Spozalizio, The virgin and child with St John the Baptist or portrait of Baldassare Castiglione.

programme.title: Video Art
programme.desc: Street art, performances, digital art... Because the new forms of Art and their invigorating character, even iconoclastic, have been able to attract a new public until now refractory to go inside the interior of our beautiful museums.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.desc: Pablo Picasso est l’un des plus grands artistes au monde, et jusqu’à sa mort en 1973, il était le plus prolifique. Comment tout a commencé ? Comment s’est construit Picasso ? Après avoir été trop longtemps ignorées, il est temps de se concentrer sur les jeunes années de l’artiste et son éducation qui lui ont permis d’accomplir l’extraordinaire. Trois villes ont joué un rôle-clé : Malaga, Barcelone et Paris. "Le jeune Picasso" les traverse et explore leur influence sur l’artiste, en analysant des œuvres spécifiques de ses débuts.

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: Museo del Prado in Madrid holds one of the biggest collections of paintings in the world. The “Garden of Delights”, a triptych by Hieronymus Bosch, is one of its jewels. But naturally it is the Spanish School which predominates, with masterpieces by Velasquez and Goya. Look at Velasquez’s famous “Las Meninas” in which the painter portrays himself next to the Infanta and her followers. And admire the sensuous beauty of Goya’s “La Maja Desnuda” or the political force of “El Tres de Mayo”.Enjoy your visit!

programme.title: Architectures
programme.desc: La Pyramide de Djoser, la plus ancienne pyramide égyptienne - 2 600 AJC révèle sans doute les tous premiers pas de ce qu'on appelle l'architecture, comme pratique savante, distincte de la simple construction. Le lieu où s'opère cette coupure est un monument funéraire, où la fonction symbolique prend le pas sur le simple usage. L'architecture à sa naissance s'affirme d'emblée non pas comme fonction mais comme signe. A trente kilomètres au sud du Caire, à l'est la vallée du Nil, Saqqarah fut la nécropole de Memphis, la première capitale de l'ancienne Égypte, dont il ne reste rien sauf cet immense cimetière dominé par les soixante mètres de la pyramide à degrés de Djoser.

programme.title: Little Sketches
programme.desc: In 64 fun episodes, Gia-Haï Phan teaches you all the sketching techniques you need.

programme.title: Sketchbook
programme.desc: Raphael Federici (street artist), Marine Goodmorning (illustrator), Luca Fiore (cartoonist) and Harry Boudchicha (drawing teacher) reveal their best drawing techniques. Each episode focuses on a specific subject:movement, oil painting, shadows, hair... For the first 10 minutes, each artist gives a theoretical lesson illustrated by sketches. Then they each start working on their creation. Finally, they end with a debrief, comparingand explaining their techniques.

programme.title: Art Quiz
programme.desc: Test your knowledge and your memory in fine arts, architecture, photography, street art and a dozen other topics with our Art Quiz popups !

programme.title: My little Museum
programme.desc: My little Museum is Art History retold through funny anecdotes and backstage trivias by Alicia Flipo. With her wacky demeanor, her inventiveness and her quirky sense of humor, Alicia stages these small Art tales so that once and for all, you could remember the Big stories!

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Le rockeur progressif Rick Wakeman et son grand ami Ian Lavender, célèbre pour son rôle de Pike dans Dad’s Army, s’offrent une joyeuse virée sur les routes secondaires du Norfolk. Accompagnés des experts Raj Bisram et David Harper, ces deux chasseurs d’antiquités sillonnent le comté à la recherche de trésors à vendre aux enchères à Nottingham.Rick découvre d’anciens graffitis médiévaux cachés dans la cathédrale de Norwich, tandis qu’Ian fait un détour vers le nord du Norfolk pour admirer la plus grande collection de soldats miniatures au monde.Mais un sage vieil homme japonais aidera-t-il Ian à triompher aux enchères ?

programme.title: Masterpiece
programme.desc: A l'époque des fastes, Coorte passait des heures à travailler sur ses natures mortes austères composées d'une botte d'asperges, de trois pêches ou d'un bol de fraises. Comment se fait-il qu'il faille huit couleurs différentes pour approcher le blanc des asperges de Coorte ? Et pourquoi sont-elles si transparentes ? La recherche des réponses conduit l'équipe dans une petite église et chez un cultivateur d'asperges dans les provinces néerlandaises, ce qui conduit à une découverte spéciale dans un laboratoire de chimie.

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: This famous art gallery in Berlin holds one of the world’s leading collections of European works by Old Masters. Here you can find “The Fountain of Youth” by Cranach the Elder, or the earthy “Netherlandish Proverbs” illustrated by Bruegel. Allow yourself to be tempted by the sensuality of Correggio (“Leda and the Swan”) or of Titian (“Venus with Organist”), by the poetry of “The Glass of Wine”, a genre painting by Vermeer, or by the gracious “Iris or the Dance” by Watteau. Enjoy your visit!

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.sub-title: Rembrandt at the National Gallery, London & Rijksmuseum, Amsterdam
programme.desc: Every Rembrandt exhibition is eagerly awaited, but this exhibition organized by London's National Gallery and Amsterdam's Rijksmuseum was an unprecedented event. The documentary highlights the historic Rembrandt exhibition, while tracing his life. Rembrandt is one of the greatest artists who ever lived, and this deeply moving documentary seeks to explore the truth about the man behind the legend.

programme.title: Sketchbook
programme.desc: Raphael Federici (street artist), Marine Goodmorning (illustrator), Luca Fiore (cartoonist) and Harry Boudchicha (drawing teacher) reveal their best drawing techniques. Each episode focuses on a specific subject:movement, oil painting, shadows, hair... For the first 10 minutes, each artist gives a theoretical lesson illustrated by sketches. Then they each start working on their creation. Finally, they end with a debrief, comparingand explaining their techniques.

programme.title: Private Tour
programme.desc: Private Tour takes you to the discovery of the retrospective "Tutto Ponti" which retraces 60 years of the career of the architect-designer Gio Ponti at the decorative arts museum. This is the first retrospective devoted to this Italian artist. You will discover his ceramic works from the beginning of his career, but also the furniture designs anchored in his poetic and subtle universe.

programme.title: Private Tour
programme.desc: Private Tour takes you to discover the exhibition "Red ! From Jean-Jacques Henner to Sonia Rikiel" at the Jean-Jacques Henner National Museum in Paris. The theme of red hair seems to be a real subject in 19th century painting. During this visit, you will be able to observe paintings by Auguste Renoir, Edgard Maxence but especially Jean-Jacques Henner and Sonia Rikiel.

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: Named Euphrosyne, Thalia and Aglaia, they embody mirth, abundance and splendour. They are the Three Graces, daughters of Jupiter and companions of Venus. A very prized subject as early as the Renaissance, it allowed for a triple representation of the female nude in all its ideal beauty. Observe their grace in the painting by Raphael and the delicacy of their veils in Botticelli’s “La Primavera” or in a magnificent work by Cranach recently acquired by the Louvre Museum. Enjoy your visit!

programme.title: Architectures
programme.desc: La Pyramide de Djoser, la plus ancienne pyramide égyptienne - 2 600 AJC révèle sans doute les tous premiers pas de ce qu'on appelle l'architecture, comme pratique savante, distincte de la simple construction. Le lieu où s'opère cette coupure est un monument funéraire, où la fonction symbolique prend le pas sur le simple usage. L'architecture à sa naissance s'affirme d'emblée non pas comme fonction mais comme signe. A trente kilomètres au sud du Caire, à l'est la vallée du Nil, Saqqarah fut la nécropole de Memphis, la première capitale de l'ancienne Égypte, dont il ne reste rien sauf cet immense cimetière dominé par les soixante mètres de la pyramide à degrés de Djoser.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.sub-title: David Hockney à la Royal Academy of Arts
programme.desc: David Hockney, généralement considéré comme l’un des artistes britanniques les plus populaires, connaît un succès mondial grâce à ses expositions à Londres, New York, Paris et ailleurs qui attirent des millions de spectateurs. Devenu récemment octogénaire, Hockney ne semble pas avoir l’intention de lever le pied ni de perdre son audace légendaire. Ce film révèle des interviews intimes et inédites et se concentre sur deux expositions à succès ayant eu lieu en 2012 et 2016 à la Royal Academy of Arts Londres. Le réalisateur Phil Grabsky a bénéficié d’un accès privilégié pour célébrer sur grand écran ce génie créatif du XXIe siècle.

programme.title: Graffiti World
programme.desc: Graffiti World is a short program dedicated to contemporary urban art around the world. Each episode explores a specific city, highlighting iconic works of street art. Local soundscape accompanies the images for an authentic, commentary-free immersion. The program showcases the creativity and diversity of artistic expression in public spaces. An invitation to discover the richness of graffiti as an art form in its own right.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.desc: Without Camille Pissarro, there is no Impressionist movement. He is rightfully known as the father of Impressionism.It was a dramatic path that Pissarro followed, and throughout it all he wrote extensively to his family. It is through these intimate and revealing letters that this gripping film reveals Pissarro’s life and work.Born in the West Indies, Pissarro found his passion in paint as a young man in Paris, and by the age of 43 had corralled a group of enthusiastic artists into a new collective. Their first show was scorned by the critics, but the group had acquired a new name: the Impressionists. For the next 40 years Pissarro was the driving force behind what has today become the world’s favourite artistic movement.The Ashmolean Museum has a long tradition of excellence. As the UK’s first ever public museum (it opened its doors in May 1683) it houses a wonderfully rich collection, including the remarkable Pissarro archive. With exclusive access to most extensive archive of.

programme.title: My little Museum
programme.desc: My little Museum is Art History retold through funny anecdotes and backstage trivias by Alicia Flipo. With her wacky demeanor, her inventiveness and her quirky sense of humor, Alicia stages these small Art tales so that once and for all, you could remember the Big stories!

programme.title: Artist's Workshop
programme.desc: Joaquin Boz habite et travaille à Buenos Aires. C’est ici, dans la capitale de l’Argentine, qu’il pratique son art à mi-chemin entre abstraction et performance corporelle. Sa peinture est recouverte de couches toujours plus nombreuses. Ces tâches, ces mouvements, ces traits et ces raclures, sont le fruit de la transmission des formes et des couleurs depuis son corps vers la toile dans une performance physique de l’artiste qui puise son inspiration dans le courant expressionniste.

programme.title: Artist's Workshop
programme.desc: In the Artist's workshop series, we offer you a privileged meeting with an artist, or the discovery of an artist or an important place for the history of art. Whether they are painters, visual artists, sculptors... These people all have the same passion for art and this show is an opportunity to enter their different universes, to discover what drives them, inspires them and maybe that will give rise to vocations.

programme.title: The decisive moment
programme.desc: In this episode, we will meet Slam, a portrait photographer. He lives between Paris and New-York, and he is a photographer specialized in beauty, music, fashion, he makes portraits sometimes provocative. He will tell us about his life, his career, and how he became a photographer.

programme.title: The decisive moment
programme.desc: Jady Bates is an internationally known photographer, exhibited at Soho Photo Gallery in New York. She is known for her "darkroom photographer" series, which depicts naked women in black and white with words and/or phrases written on them. She is also the photographer of the band THE SLANTS.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Le rockeur progressif Rick Wakeman et son grand ami Ian Lavender, célèbre pour son rôle de Pike dans Dad’s Army, s’offrent une joyeuse virée sur les routes secondaires du Norfolk. Accompagnés des experts Raj Bisram et David Harper, ces deux chasseurs d’antiquités sillonnent le comté à la recherche de trésors à vendre aux enchères à Nottingham.Rick découvre d’anciens graffitis médiévaux cachés dans la cathédrale de Norwich, tandis qu’Ian fait un détour vers le nord du Norfolk pour admirer la plus grande collection de soldats miniatures au monde.Mais un sage vieil homme japonais aidera-t-il Ian à triompher aux enchères ?

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Les chefs télévisés Aldo Zilli et Ching He-Huang s’affrontent lors de ce Road Trip à travers le sud-est de l’Angleterre, accompagnés des experts Natasha Raskin et Paul Laidlaw.Aldo mise sur une bague en or malgré les conseils des experts, tandis que Ching espère que des antiquités d’origine orientale feront un beau score aux enchères dans le nord de Londres.En chemin, Aldo fait une halte pour rencontrer Jill, une doyenne de 200 ans qui fournissait autrefois la population en farine. De son côté, l’amoureuse des animaux Ching découvre l’histoire d’un homme qui, au XIXe siècle, a risqué le ridicule et la ruine pour protéger le bétail.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.desc: Phil Grabsky, réalisateur récompensé à plusieurs reprises, pose un regard neuf sur celui qui est sans conteste l’artiste le plus aimé au monde en utilisant les mots du peintre lui-même. Moi, Claude Monet révèle de nouvelles facettes de l’homme qui a donné naissance au mouvement impressionniste grâce à un tableau et qui fut certainement l’un des peintres les plus influents et renommés du XIXe et du début du XXe siècle. Tourné à travers l’Europe, sur les lieux où il a peint, Moi, Claude Monet est une exploration filmique inédite et intime de certains des décors cultes les plus appréciés de l’art occidental.

programme.title: Art contest
programme.desc: With just one subject and 45 minutes on the clock, four artists go head-to-head in a public showdown of creativity. In the heart of Le Havre, they must create a unique artwork on the spot, turning a simple theme into a masterpiece. Will they rise to the challenge or crumble under pressure?

programme.title: Le Rendez-vous du dimanche
programme.desc: Every first sunday of the month, Museum TV puts a spotlight on an artist, an artistic current or a famous work of art! In June, follow the footsteps of a master of Flemish art.

programme.title: Private Tour
programme.sub-title: A landscape of excellence, three figures of French architecture
programme.desc: Private Tour takes you to discover the exhibition "A landscape of excellence, three figures of French architecture" at the Cité de l'Architecture & du Patrimoine in Paris. This exhibition offers a journey through contemporary creation. Since the 90s, three figures of French architecture have marked the history of art, Frédéric Borel, Marc Barani and Jean-Marc Ibos and Myrto Vitart. They have shown the excellence of French creations in the world.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.desc: Lucian Freud, l'un des peintres britanniques les plus célèbres de notre époque, est également l'un des rares artistes du XXe siècle à s'être représenté dans un autoportrait avec une telle constance. Ses autoportraits donnent un aperçu fascinant à la fois de sa personnalité et de son évolution en tant que peintre, depuis son premier portrait peint en 1939 jusqu'au dernier. Grâce à des entretiens fascinants avec d'anciens modèles, des amis et d'éminents experts, ce documentaire intensément captivant révèle l'œuvre d'un maître qui représente une étude captivante sur la dynamique du vieillissement et le processus d'autoreprésentation.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.sub-title: Le curieux monde de Hieronymus Bosch
programme.desc: La ville natale de Bosch aux Pays-Bas ne possède pas de tableaux du peintre et pourtant, pour la grande exposition « Visions of Genius » qui a eu lieu à l’anniversaire de sa mort en 2016, le musée Noordbrabants a réussi à réunir la presque totalité des tableaux et dessins de Bosch. C’est en 2008 que le directeur du musée a commencé sa tournée dans les plus grands musées d’Europe et Etats-Unis pour en persuader les directeurs de prêter au musée Noordbrabants leurs œuvres, en échange d’un travail d’analyse et de recherche sur les tableaux en prêt. Ainsi « Hieronymus Bosch. Visions of Genius » a été l’exposition la plus ambitieuse dédiée au peintre hollandais. Ce documentaire sera une plongée dans l’univers étrange et visionnaire de Hieronymus Bosch.

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: Discover or re-discover the masterpieces of the greatest painters of history through our documentary series Within The Frame. From impressionists to surrealists, from Leonard Da Vinci to Paul Cézanne, every episode will immerge you into a virtual tour of a paintings collection. You will see the most impressive paintings ever made and won't miss a single detail.

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: This famous art gallery in Berlin holds one of the world’s leading collections of European works by Old Masters. Here you can find “The Fountain of Youth” by Cranach the Elder, or the earthy “Netherlandish Proverbs” illustrated by Bruegel. Allow yourself to be tempted by the sensuality of Correggio (“Leda and the Swan”) or of Titian (“Venus with Organist”), by the poetry of “The Glass of Wine”, a genre painting by Vermeer, or by the gracious “Iris or the Dance” by Watteau. Enjoy your visit!

programme.title: Art Quiz
programme.desc: Test your knowledge and your memory in fine arts, architecture, photography, street art and a dozen other topics with our Art Quiz popups !

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Strictly Come Dancing’s Zoe Ball and Ian Waite are quickstepping their way across the south West of England in a bid to turn a profit at auction in Hampshire. Experts Margie Cooper and Paul Laidlaw are on hand to guide. Paul shares a secret with Zoe about buying antique hair brushes. Ian, though, offers dancing lessons to dealers to secure a discount. Lordy! Zoe also hears how one village became world famous for lace production while Ian ends up meeting a giant horse with its own boat.

programme.title: Photo Contest
programme.desc: In each episode of Photo Contest, two contestants compete on an imposed theme such as rock'n roll, Japan or architecture. They have one hour to take the best possible photographs before editing them for 30 minutes in a studio. The photographs they have selected are then presented to Agnès Grégoire, editor-in-chief of PHOTO magazine, who will have to choose the best photographer.

programme.title: Artist's Workshop
programme.desc: In the Artist's workshop series, we offer you a privileged meeting with an artist, or the discovery of an artist or an important place for the history of art. Whether they are painters, visual artists, sculptors... These people all have the same passion for art and this series is an opportunity to enter their different universes, to discover what drives them, inspires them and maybe that will give rise to vocations.

programme.title: An hour with
programme.desc: Take an hour break to discover the work of a Franco-Danish painter, Camille Pissarro. You will discover his labour with View from Louveciennes, A path through the fields, Pontoise or Peasant girl making a fire.

programme.title: An hour with
programme.desc: Take an hour break to discover the work of a Prussian painter, Caspar David Friedrich. You will discover his labour with The Wanderer above the mists, Greifswald port or the Lone tree.

programme.title: Video Art
programme.desc: Street art, performances, digital art... Because the new forms of Art and their invigorating character, even iconoclastic, have been able to attract a new public until now refractory to go inside the interior of our beautiful museums.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.desc: « J’envie les Japonais », écrivait Van Gogh à son frère, Théo. Grâce à l’exposition sur laquelle est basé ce film, « Van Gogh et le Japon », au musée Van Gogh d’Amsterdam, on comprend pourquoi. Bien que Vincent van Gogh n’ait jamais visité le Japon, il reste le pays qui a eu le plus d’influence sur lui et son œuvre. En se rendant dans les galeries d’art japonais à Paris et en créant sa propre image du Japon après des recherches approfondies, des collections d’estampes et de longues discussions avec d’autres artistes, la découverte des œuvres japonaises entraîna la carrière de Van Gogh dans une nouvelle direction très excitante.

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: This gallery was built in the 16th century as the seat for Florence’s new administrative offices (uffizi), and opened to the public as the Uffizi Gallery in the 18th century. Today it still offers the world’s most beautiful collection of Italian Renaissance art. How could anyone remain insensitive to the sensual beauty of “The Birth of Venus” by Botticelli, “The Venus of Urbino” by Titian, or Correggio’s “Danae”? Enjoy your visit!

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: Jean-François Millet (1814 - 1875) was a 19th-century French realist painter, pastellist, engraver and draughtsman. He is best known for his realistic rural scenes.

programme.title: Sketchbook
programme.desc: Raphael Federici (street artist), Marine Goodmorning (illustrator), Luca Fiore (cartoonist) and Harry Boudchicha (drawing teacher) reveal their best drawing techniques. Each episode focuses on a specific subject:movement, oil painting, shadows, hair... For the first 10 minutes, each artist gives a theoretical lesson illustrated by sketches. Then they each start working on their creation. Finally, they end with a debrief, comparingand explaining their techniques.

programme.title: Little Sketches
programme.desc: In 64 fun episodes, Gia-Haï Phan teaches you all the sketching techniques you need.

programme.title: Architectures
programme.desc: A tall tower in the heart of Copenhagen, the RADISSON SAS ROYAL HOTEL is both a grand modern hotel and an airline terminal, marking Denmark's entry into post-war modernity. It was a major work of the 1950s, under the banner of functionalism, simplicity and elegance. This total work of art, where everything from the ashtray to the volume of the building was conceived and designed by the same man, Arne Jacobsen, the great Scandinavian architect and designer.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.desc: « Les Tournesols » sont parmi les tableaux de Van Gogh les plus célèbres. Le musée Van Gogh à Amsterdam a consacré une exposition à ces œuvres et « Exhibition on screen », qui a eu un accès exclusif à l’événement, nous guide à travers l’exposition pour enquêter sur les mystères qui entourent ces tableaux. Le film nous amènera également à Tokyo, Philadelphie, Londres et Munich pour suivre les 5 principales œuvres de l’exposition.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.sub-title: Goya: Visions of Flesh and Blood
programme.desc: Francisco Goya, considéré comme le fondateur de l’art moderne, est l’artiste espagnol le plus acclamé. En plus d’observer avec talent la vie et le passé mouvementé de l’Espagne, c’est un portraitiste doué et un commentateur social par excellence. Avec Goya, l’art du portrait atteint de nouveaux sommets. Cette exposition majeure de la National Gallery de Londres remet son génie en lumière. À travers une formidable collection d’oeuvres uniques et de lettres de Goya, nous analysons l’homme lui-même et le monde qu’il peignait.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.desc: En juin 2017, une grande exposition a ouvert à Paris au musée d’Orsay. Pour la première fois, cette exposition a réuni tous les portraits de Cézanne. Cela a été l’occasion d’analyser l’œuvre de Cézanne à l’aide d’experts internationaux et de parcourir les salles de la National Portrait Gallery de Londres et de la National Gallery of Art de Washington.

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: In this episode we will decipher the most famous works of Johannes Vermeer. He was born in 1632 and lived his whole life in Delft. He painted mainly interior scenes, everyday life with middle-class people but also with servants. He is the author of "The Milkmaid

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: How can desire be expressed in painting? Over the centuries, numerous artists have passionately sought to express or arouse such a sentiment. Take a look at paintings such as “Rolla” by Gerveix representing the total abandonment of the woman’s body. The story of “Danae”, painted by Corregio, provides a mythological excuse for depicting the female body naked. As for Fragonard, he succeeded in reproducing the acme of desire between man and woman as they struggle around the “Bolt”. Enjoy your visit!

programme.title: My little Museum
programme.desc: My little Museum is Art History retold through funny anecdotes and backstage trivias by Alicia Flipo. With her wacky demeanor, her inventiveness and her quirky sense of humor, Alicia stages these small Art tales so that once and for all, you could remember the Big stories!

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actor and charmer Nigel Havers and his canny, deal-making chum (and ex-agent) Michael Whitehall explore the West Country. Travelling by Bentley and TVR, they’ll have £400 each and expert advice from Margie Cooper and James Braxton. But what will do best at the auction... Nigel’s old cockle bucket or Michael’s tambourine? Plus there’s a trip to see some Bristol Savages and bit of Georgian elegance with the story of ‘The King of Bath’.

programme.title: A special day
programme.desc: In this episode, we will spend a special day with Fanny Bouyagui, visual artist and director of the association Art Point M, and who is at the origin of the Braderie de l'Art in Roubaix. Since 1991, this event welcomes dozens of artists with a single motto, create from recycled objects. But before the arrival of the public, Fanny must make sure that certain rules are respected. We will accompany her throughout this very busy day.

programme.title: Museum stories
programme.desc: Throughout the first half of the 20th century, the Marmottan Museum was a center of First Empire art. We go there to admire the magnificent collection of Paul Marmottan, and to relive the nostalgia of the great Napoleonic era, its imperial splendor, its past glories. But little by little, the Marmottan Museum is becoming a showcase for another style of art, less classic, more avant-garde: impressionism. In 1993, the museum changed its name: it became the Musée Marmottan-Monet.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedy legend Jennifer Saunders and best pal, top actress Patricia Potter take to the road on a humdinger of a trip. Joined by veteran antiquers Phillip Serrell and Mark Stacey they shop in Berkshire, Hampshire and Oxfordshire before heading for an auction in Greenwich, London. Jennifer and Phil detour to hear about Fred Karno, the man credited with inventing slapstick comedy. While Patricia and Mark learn how a surgeon from Reading used his incredible sense of smell to successfully treat soldiers injured in World War One.And, Phil gets custard pied – what’s not to love?

programme.title: Artist's Workshop
programme.desc: In the Artist's workshop series, we offer you a privileged meeting with an artist, or the discovery of an artist or an important place for the history of art. Whether they are painters, visual artists, sculptors... These people all have the same passion for art and this show is an opportunity to enter their different universes, to discover what drives them, inspires them and maybe that will give rise to vocations.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.sub-title: Léonard à la National Gallery de Londres
programme.desc: Un événement remarquable s'est produit à la National Gallery de Londres lorsque la plus grande collection jamais constituée de peintures de Léonard de Vinci a été réunie dans une exposition unique : Léonard de Vinci : peintre à la cour de Milan. Léonard est reconnu comme étant peut-être le plus grand peintre et sculpteur de tous les temps, mais ses talents ne s'arrêtaient pas là. Ce film raconte sa vie et s'étend sur Léonard le visionnaire, Léonard l'inventeur. Depuis l'endroit où il a vécu ses dernières années au Château du Clos Lucé en France, nous verrons que Léonard de Vinci était peut-être l'incarnation même de l'esprit de la Renaissance.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.desc: Narrated by Gillian Anderson, this episode of Exhibition on Screen follows the footsteps of French artists such as Renoir and Monet, the American Impressionist movement forged its own path. Painted over a period of 40 years, these paintings reveal the United States both as a nation and as a creative breeding ground. The history of this movement is closely linked to the love of gardens and the desire to preserve nature in a country undergoing rapid urbanization. This enchanting journey through workshops, gardens and famous sites in the USA, UK and France is a feast for the eyes.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Deux légendes de la comédie britannique, Ronni Ancona et Jan Ravens, sont accompagnées des antiquaires Phil Serrell et James Braxton pour ce Road Trip. Partant de la ville de Redcar, dans le North Yorkshire, ils se dirigent vers une vente aux enchères à Stockport, dans le Cheshire.Jan se laisse emporter et dépense tout son budget, tandis que Ronni se régale d'une paire de bottes en verre très kitsch.Lors d'un détour, Jan découvre pourquoi les habitants de Hartlepool pensaient qu'un singe était un Français, et Ronni reste bouche bée lorsque Braxton enlève ses vêtements. Sacré moment !

programme.title: The decisive moment
programme.desc: Lissa Rivera is a Brooklyn-based photographer whose work has received numerous awards and accolades and has been exhibited internationally. Lissa's latest project looks at photography's connection to identity on a personal level, focusing on her domestic partner as her muse. Rivera was chosen as a "Woman to Watch" for the biennial exhibition at the National Museum of Women in the Arts.

programme.title: Private Tour
programme.sub-title: Emil Bührle collection at the Maillol Museum
programme.desc: Private Tour takes you to discover the Emil Bührle collection at the Maillol Museum in Paris.This collection is that of Emil Bührle, a student of art history who became passionate about collecting Monet's works after a visit to the National Gallery in Berlin. He started his collection with works by Claude Monet, but continued to fill it with works by Cézanne, Gauguin... Works that you can observe during this visit.

programme.title: Private Tour
programme.desc: Private tour takes you to discover the exhibition "Japan-japanism, inspired objects, 1867-2018" at the decorative arts museum. Japan began to be known around the 1850s and its art was quickly recognized. In this exhibition you will be able to observe Japanese objects, but also objects of Japanese influence which testify to the relations between France and Japan.

programme.title: Sketchbook
programme.desc: Raphael Federici (street artist), Marine Goodmorning (illustrator), Luca Fiore (cartoonist) and Harry Boudchicha (drawing teacher) reveal their best drawing techniques. Each episode focuses on a specific subject:movement, oil painting, shadows, hair... For the first 10 minutes, each artist gives a theoretical lesson illustrated by sketches. Then they each start working on their creation. Finally, they end with a debrief, comparingand explaining their techniques.

programme.title: Masterpiece
programme.desc: A l'époque des fastes, Coorte passait des heures à travailler sur ses natures mortes austères composées d'une botte d'asperges, de trois pêches ou d'un bol de fraises. Comment se fait-il qu'il faille huit couleurs différentes pour approcher le blanc des asperges de Coorte ? Et pourquoi sont-elles si transparentes ? La recherche des réponses conduit l'équipe dans une petite église et chez un cultivateur d'asperges dans les provinces néerlandaises, ce qui conduit à une découverte spéciale dans un laboratoire de chimie.

programme.title: Artist's Workshop
programme.desc: In the Artist's workshop series, we offer you a privileged meeting with an artist, or the discovery of an artist or an important place for the history of art. Whether they are painters, visual artists, sculptors... These people all have the same passion for art and this show is an opportunity to enter their different universes, to discover what drives them, inspires them and maybe that will give rise to vocations.

programme.title: Masterpieces Unveiled
programme.desc: Where do we start when we want to talk about such a famous painting as Velázquez “Las Meninas”? There is an incredibly huge bibliography on this painting, to the point that it is almost utopian to think or hope we can document ourselves on everything in and about it... From the late 1800s to the early 2000s, dozens of books have come out all over the world specifically and solely devoted to this one, single painting. A parallel literary current has even developed around the “enigmas” and “mysteries” connected with “Las Meninas”.

programme.title: A special day
programme.desc: In this episode, we will spend a special day with Jef Aerosol, one of the pioneers of street art. This stencil artist is the sponsor of Solid'Art, a contemporary art fair unlike any other, where the money raised helps children go on vacation. To celebrate the opening of the fair, he worked on a performance. We will follow him throughout this day of preparations.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.desc: The National Gallery in London is one of the world’s greatest art galleries. It is brimming with masterpieces, an inexhaustible treasure trove of history and an endless source of stories. This film gives a voice to those whose lives have been touched by the Gallery, from the security guard to the director, including daily visitors, celebrities and members of the royal family. Claudia Winkleman, Michael Palin, Terry Gilliam, Jacqueline Wilson and Her Royal Highness Princess Eugenie reveal their deeply personal connection to the National Gallery.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.desc: Qui était Frida Kahlo ? Voyagez dans la vie d'une véritable icône, découvrez son art et la vérité qui se cache derrière sa vie souvent mouvementée. La combinaison d'interviews, de commentaires et d'une exploration détaillée de l'art de Kahlo, offre un trésor de couleurs et un festin de vitalité. Ce documentaire personnel et intime offre un accès privilégié à ses œuvres, et met en lumière sa créativité fébrile, sa résilience et sa soif inégalée pour la vie.

programme.title: Exhibition on Screen
programme.desc: Phil Grabsky, réalisateur récompensé à plusieurs reprises, pose un regard neuf sur celui qui est sans conteste l’artiste le plus aimé au monde en utilisant les mots du peintre lui-même. Moi, Claude Monet révèle de nouvelles facettes de l’homme qui a donné naissance au mouvement impressionniste grâce à un tableau et qui fut certainement l’un des peintres les plus influents et renommés du XIXe et du début du XXe siècle. Tourné à travers l’Europe, sur les lieux où il a peint, Moi, Claude Monet est une exploration filmique inédite et intime de certains des décors cultes les plus appréciés de l’art occidental.

programme.title: Art Quiz
programme.desc: Test your knowledge and your memory in fine arts, architecture, photography, street art and a dozen other topics with our Art Quiz popups !

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: Paul Sérusier spent his entire summer of 1888 painting with Paul Gauguin in Pont-Aven, Brittany. This master of colour gave invaluable advice to the young Sérusier, who was part of the new generation of painters known as the Post-Impressionists. The Impressionists favoured the visual sensation of the motif, but they had not broken with the illusionist rendering of nature.

programme.title: Within the frame
programme.desc: Named Euphrosyne, Thalia and Aglaia, they embody mirth, abundance and splendour. They are the Three Graces, daughters of Jupiter and companions of Venus. A very prized subject as early as the Renaissance, it allowed for a triple representation of the female nude in all its ideal beauty. Observe their grace in the painting by Raphael and the delicacy of their veils in Botticelli’s “La Primavera” or in a magnificent work by Cranach recently acquired by the Louvre Museum. Enjoy your visit!

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Le rockeur progressif Rick Wakeman et son grand ami Ian Lavender, célèbre pour son rôle de Pike dans Dad’s Army, s’offrent une joyeuse virée sur les routes secondaires du Norfolk. Accompagnés des experts Raj Bisram et David Harper, ces deux chasseurs d’antiquités sillonnent le comté à la recherche de trésors à vendre aux enchères à Nottingham.Rick découvre d’anciens graffitis médiévaux cachés dans la cathédrale de Norwich, tandis qu’Ian fait un détour vers le nord du Norfolk pour admirer la plus grande collection de soldats miniatures au monde.Mais un sage vieil homme japonais aidera-t-il Ian à triompher aux enchères ?

programme.title: Photo Contest
programme.desc: In each episode of Photo Contest, two contestants compete on an imposed theme such as rock'n roll, Japan or architecture. They have one hour to take the best possible photographs before editing them for 30 minutes in a studio. The photographs they have selected are then presented to Agnès Grégoire, editor-in-chief of PHOTO magazine, who will have to choose the best photographer.

programme.title: Artist's Workshop
programme.desc: Gutenberg est considéré comme le père de l’imprimerie moderne. La petite histoire raconte qu’il se serait inspiré du pressoir à raisin utilisé par les vignerons pour inventer sa machine. De cette anecdote historique, deux artistes ont entrepris d’éditer un livre qui étanchera la soif de n’importe quel lecteur gourmand. Dans cet épisode, nous rencontrerons Luz et Anaïs, deux artistes opposées et absolument complémentaires.

programme.title: An hour with
programme.desc: Take an hour break to discover the work of a Spanish painter, Francisco de Goya. You will discover his labour with Lunch in the Countryside, The third of may 1808 or the Cannibals.

programme.title: An hour with
programme.desc: Take an hour break to discover the work of a Flemish painter, Pierre Paul Rubens. You will discover his labour with Samson and Dalilah, The four philosophers or The education of Marie de’Medici.

programme.title: Video Art
programme.desc: Street art, performances, digital art... Because the new forms of Art and their invigorating character, even iconoclastic, have been able to attract a new public until now refractory to go inside the interior of our beautiful museums.
